title: penducella nummularia
intro: Penducella nummularia, also known as buttonbush, is a flowering shrub native to North America, characterized by its spherical flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and sore throats. The health benefits of buttonbush include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, buttonbush is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in salves or ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
